evals.name: sv-edit-article-polish
evals.expected_output: Polished Swedish article — mechanics and style applied directly, no user dialogue.
expectations: The main agent settles the finding list directly without invoking a subagent (default `--max-iterations=0`).
Genre resolves to `article` via standard flow (H1 + standfirst + byline present).
Mechanics (Phase 1) and style (Phase 2) findings are both applied to the polished output.
*Marx teorier* and *CoMaps föregångare* retain the correct zero-marking after polishing.

evals.name: sv-edit-press-release-attribution
evals.expected_output: Polished Swedish press release with speech-dash attribution and Swedish outer quotation marks.
expectations: Attributed speech is converted to speech-dash form (talstreck — EN-dash with space) or, where verbatim wording matters, kept in ”…” marks.
Quotation marks are normalised to ”…” (Swedish outer).
Genre resolves to `press-release` via the prompt trigger.
The polished text preserves the news content and the attributed source.

evals.name: sv-edit-with-max-iterations-deep-natural-language
evals.prompt: /edit sv kör djupt på den här texten
evals.expected_output: The natural-language phrase *kör djupt* maps to --max-iterations=3 and the subagent loop is invoked.
expectations: The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 3 iterations.
The natural-language phrase *kör djupt* is parsed to 3 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=3` flag.
The subagent's convergence rules still apply — early consensus stops the loop before 3 rounds if main and subagent agree.
No user dialogue happens in Phase 3 — `/edit` is the AFK variant.

evals.name: sv-edit-ai-tells-and-anglicisms-applied
evals.expected_output: Polished output replaces AI-tells and anglicisms with Swedish substitutes.
expectations: *Det är värt att notera att…*, *I en värld där…*, *Sammanfattningsvis*, *Dyk djupare* are removed or rewritten.

*adressera* becomes *ta upp* or *behandla*; *navigera* becomes *hantera*; *leverera* becomes *ge* or *erbjuda*; *metrik* becomes *mätvärde* or *nyckeltal*; *regulatorer* becomes *myndigheter* or *tillsynsmyndigheter*.
Split-compound errors are corrected as part of Phase 1.

evals.name: sv-edit-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Polished version shortens over-long headings while preserving meaning.
expectations: The H1 is shortened to a readable length (under 60 characters where the meaning allows).
At least one over-long H2 is shortened.

Body changes are traceable to Phase 2 findings — no silent rewrites. Sentences and paragraphs not flagged by any finding in `## Findings` remain substantively unchanged in `output.md`. Findings-driven restructuring (e.g. ABT-shaped lead, standfirst rework, broken-sentence repair) is in scope; unprompted prose changes are not.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` Phase 2 documents that the style-layer review was applied. Any one of these is sufficient evidence: (a) the path `lib/rules/style.md` appears in the Phase 2 entry, (b) the Phase 2 entry uses phrasing that names the layer (e.g. "style layers", "style-layer pass", "style-layer review", or a close variant), or (c) the Phase 2 entry enumerates two or more of style.md's section categories — precision, rhythm / transitions, AI-tells / constructions, cognitive load / pedagogy, address / voice — as review steps.

evals.name: sv-edit-fabricated-source-flagged-as-closing-note
evals.expected_output: Polished text retains structure; suspicious source citations are flagged as a closing note for the writer to verify.
expectations: Cited sources that look fabricated are preserved verbatim in the polished output (`/edit` does not invent or correct facts).
A closing note alerts the writer that one or more sources need verification, naming the specific lines or citations.
Attributed quotes preserve their attribution (the named researcher's name is not changed).

evals.name: en_GB-edit-article-polish
evals.expected_output: Polished British English article with mechanics and style applied directly.
expectations: Genre resolves to `article` via standard flow.
British conventions are applied: single outer curly quotes, no Oxford comma, DD/MM/YYYY dates, EN-dash with spaces for parenthetical insertion.
Attribution uses single curly quotes (British convention), not speech dashes (which are Swedish).
The main agent settles the findings directly without invoking a subagent.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-style-cleanup
evals.expected_output: Polished output strips AI-tell vocabulary and tightens the prose.
expectations: *In a world where…*, *it's worth noting that*, *Let me be perfectly clear* are removed.
*delve into*, *leverage* (verb), *multifaceted*, *tapestry*, *robust*, *comprehensive*, *ultimately*, *streamline* are replaced or removed.
*Furthermore*/*moreover* as paragraph crutches are removed; paragraph structure is tightened.
*To summarize* opening a conclusion is rewritten.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-with-max-iterations-flag
evals.expected_output: Subagent loop runs with a ceiling of 2 iterations.
expectations: The `--max-iterations=2` flag is honoured.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 2.
The subagent's convergence rules still apply — early consensus stops the loop before 2 rounds if main and subagent agree.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-american-drift-corrected
evals.expected_output: Polished British English with -ise/-isation spellings, BrE idioms, and BrE date order.
expectations: *organize* → *organise*; *analyze* → *analyse*; *finalize* → *finalise*.
*color* → *colour*; *favorite* → *favourite*; *center* → *centre*; *traveler* → *traveller*.
*Going forward* → *in future* or omitted.
*Leverage* (verb) → *use*, *exploit*, or *draw on*.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Headings shortened; body preserved.
expectations: The H1 is shortened to a readable length.

Body changes are traceable to Phase 2 findings — no silent rewrites. Sentences and paragraphs not flagged by any finding in `## Findings` remain substantively unchanged in `output.md`. Findings-driven restructuring (e.g. ABT-shaped lead, standfirst rework, broken-sentence repair) is in scope; unprompted prose changes are not.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` Phase 2 documents that the style-layer review was applied. Any one of these is sufficient evidence: (a) the path `lib/rules/style.md` appears in the Phase 2 entry, (b) the Phase 2 entry uses phrasing that names the layer (e.g. "style layers", "style-layer pass", "style-layer review", or a close variant), or (c) the Phase 2 entry enumerates two or more of style.md's section categories — precision, rhythm / transitions, AI-tells / constructions, cognitive load / pedagogy, address / voice — as review steps.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-fabricated-source-preserved-with-note
evals.expected_output: Polished text preserves source citations verbatim; a closing note asks the writer to verify the citations.
expectations: Cited sources are preserved verbatim in the polished output.
A closing note flags the specific citations as needing verification.
Attributed quotes preserve attribution (researcher's name and quote stay together).

evals.name: en_US-edit-article-polish
evals.expected_output: Polished American English article with mechanics and style applied directly.
expectations: Genre resolves to `article` via standard flow.
American conventions are applied: double outer curly quotes, Oxford comma in series, MM/DD/YYYY dates, EM-dash without spaces for parenthetical insertion.
Attribution uses double curly quotes (American convention), not speech dashes.
The main agent settles the findings directly without invoking a subagent (default `--max-iterations=0`).

evals.name: en_US-edit-style-cleanup
evals.expected_output: Polished output strips AI-tell vocabulary and tightens the prose.
expectations: *In a world where…*, *it's worth noting that*, *Let me be perfectly clear* are removed.
*delve into*, *leverage* (verb), *multifaceted*, *tapestry*, *robust*, *comprehensive*, *ultimately*, *unprecedented*, *streamline* are replaced or removed.
*game-changer*, *paradigm shift*, *holistic approach*, *circle back* are removed or replaced.
Polished output uses Oxford comma in series.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation. The without-skill baseline does not produce these structured records — the skill's value is the protocol fidelity behind the corrections, not the corrections alone.

evals.name: en_US-edit-quotation-applied
evals.expected_output: Polished version normalises to American conventions for quotation marks and punctuation placement.
expectations: Outer quotation marks use "…" (double curly); inner '…' (single curly).
Commas and full stops are placed inside the closing quote regardless of whether they belong to the quoted material.
Question marks belonging to the spoken utterance stay inside the quotation.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation. The without-skill baseline does not produce these structured records — the skill's value is the protocol fidelity behind the corrections, not the corrections alone.

evals.name: en_US-edit-with-max-iterations-flag-three
evals.expected_output: Subagent loop runs with a ceiling of 3 iterations.
expectations: The `--max-iterations=3` flag is honoured.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 3.
Early consensus stops the loop before 3 rounds if main and subagent agree.
Values above 3 would be clamped to 3 (protocol maximum).

evals.name: en_US-edit-typography-applied
evals.expected_output: Polished version applies American typography conventions throughout.
expectations: Thousands separator is the comma; percent in figures has no space.
Parenthetical insertion uses EM-dash without spaces.
Dates are MM/DD/YYYY; time is 12-hour with AM/PM and colon.
Oxford comma is present in series; abbreviations carry full stops (*Mr.*, *Dr.*).
British -ise/-isation drift is corrected to American -ize/-ization.

evals.name: en_US-edit-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Headings shortened; body preserved.
expectations: The H1 is shortened to a readable length.
At least one over-long H2 is shortened.

Body changes are traceable to Phase 2 findings — no silent rewrites. Sentences and paragraphs not flagged by any finding in `## Findings` remain substantively unchanged in `output.md`. Findings-driven restructuring (e.g. ABT-shaped lead, standfirst rework, broken-sentence repair) is in scope; unprompted prose changes are not.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` Phase 2 documents that the style-layer review was applied. Any one of these is sufficient evidence: (a) the path `lib/rules/style.md` appears in the Phase 2 entry, (b) the Phase 2 entry uses phrasing that names the layer (e.g. "style layers", "style-layer pass", "style-layer review", or a close variant), or (c) the Phase 2 entry enumerates two or more of style.md's section categories — precision, rhythm / transitions, AI-tells / constructions, cognitive load / pedagogy, address / voice — as review steps.

evals.name: en_GB-negative-clean-essay
evals.expected_output: A clean British English essay-style text. Edit produces a polished version with minimal substantive changes; protocol fidelity is the testable signal, not the volume of edits.
expectations: `## Findings` raises at most a small number of substantive items (no wall of false positives on clean prose).
British conventions are preserved: -ise spellings, no Oxford comma in standard series, single quotation marks, £ currency, metric units with no-break space (`21 °C`, `320 square metres`).

`## Genre / technique resolution` commits to a defensible genre for the structural markers present — `article`, `column`, or `general` are all acceptable on H1 + standfirst — and names a technique if the genre's `default_technique` is set.
`## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation.
Any change applied in the edit is traceable to a finding in the transcript — no silent rewrites.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-readme-github-boilerplate-restored
evals.expected_output: Polished GitHub README whose two drifted boilerplate sections are restored to the genre's fixed wording verbatim, with canonical structure and British conventions preserved.
expectations: `## Genre / technique resolution` commits to `readme-github` (default_technique `none`).

The *Questions, bugs, and feature requests* body is restored to the genre's fixed wording verbatim, owner/repo substituted to acme/tinycache: a paragraph `Have a usage question or something to discuss? Please use [Discussions](https://github.com/acme/tinycache/discussions).` followed by a paragraph `Found a bug or want to request a feature? Please [open an issue](https://github.com/acme/tinycache/issues). Search the existing issues first to avoid duplicates.` — the fixture's single reworded list paragraph is replaced.

The *Changelog* section's drifted line is restored to exactly `The project follows [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/) and [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).` — both Markdown links present (the fixture had dropped them and used *adheres to*).
The canonical section order and heading levels are preserved; the user-facing sections still precede *How you can contribute*.
British conventions are preserved (-ise spellings, single quotation marks, *Licence*); every applied change is traceable to a finding in the transcript — no silent rewrites.

evals.name: max-iterations-natural-language-edit
evals.prompt: /edit sv max två rundor på den här artikeln
evals.expected_output: Phase 3 invokes the subagent with a ceiling of 2 (natural-language *max två rundor* → 2).
expectations: The natural-language phrase *max två rundor* is parsed to 2 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=2` flag.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 2.
The subagent's convergence rules still apply — early consensus stops the loop before 2 rounds.
Output is the polished text, with no user-facing dialogue (the AFK variant).

evals.name: last-resort-floor-raises-to-one-when-flag-zero
evals.expected_output: Despite `--max-iterations=0`, the subagent floor is raised to 1 because the redline pass produces the last-resort developmental finding.
expectations: The redline pass identifies that the draft is below the line-editing repair threshold (vague claims, no concrete content, no hook, no source) and produces the last-resort finding from `lib/protocols/redline.md`.
The subagent floor is raised to 1 even though the flag is set to 0 — one round to sanity-check the observation.
The last-resort observation is delivered as a closing note to the user (e.g., *consider rewriting from richer source material*).
The main agent does not silently rewrite the developmental issues — those are escalated, not auto-fixed.
